title: Daily Market Digest: May 29, 2026
subtitle: Market sentiment positive amid ceasefire hopes; Indices set new records

items.headline: U.S. Stocks Extend Records, Sentiment Remains in 'Greed' Zone
items.summary: U.S. stock markets continued their upward trajectory, with major indices closing at record highs on the previous trading day. Investor sentiment remains positive, driven by hopes of a U.S.-Iran ceasefire extension and robust corporate earnings. While pre-market futures for the S&P 500 were slightly softer this morning, the overall market outlook leans towards optimism as geopolitical tensions ease and AI-driven growth continues to fuel specific sectors.

items.statContext: +0.6% on Thursday, May 28, reaching an all-time high.
+0.9% on Thursday, May 28, hitting a record closing high.
+0.05% (+24.69 points) on Thursday, May 28, also setting a new record.
Edging down around 0.1% this morning, May 29.

items.headline: Best Buy Surges on Upbeat Q1 Results
items.summary: Shares of electronics retailer Best Buy Co. Inc. surged significantly after posting upbeat first-quarter results and issuing strong guidance.

items.headline: Dollar Tree Rises on Strong Profits
items.summary: Dollar Tree's stock soared after the company reported fatter profits than analysts expected, with improved store conditions contributing to the strong performance.

items.headline: MongoDB Rallies on Earnings Beat
items.summary: MongoDB Inc.'s stock rallied following higher-than-expected quarterly earnings.

items.headline: Dell Soars on AI Data Center Demand
items.summary: Dell Technologies Inc. surged after hours, driven by raised annual revenue and profit guidance, reinforcing the robust spending on AI infrastructure.

items.headline: Zhongchao Inc. Leads Decline
items.summary: Zhongchao Inc. experienced a significant drop in share price.

items.headline: SU Group Holdings Limited Falls
items.summary: SU Group Holdings Limited saw a substantial decrease in its stock value.

items.headline: Photronics Inc. Declines
items.summary: Photronics, Inc. was among the day's biggest losers.

items.headline: Smart Powerr Corp. Experiences Sharp Drop
items.summary: Smart Powerr Corp. recorded a notable decline in its stock price.

items.headline: Key Economic Data Shows Mixed Signals on Inflation and Growth
items.summary: The U.S. core PCE price index for April rose 0.2% month-on-month, slightly below market estimates, but inflation concerns persist as the Fed's preferred gauge hit a three-year high. Meanwhile, U.S. Q1 GDP growth was revised lower to an annualized 1.6%, indicating a cooling economy. New orders for U.S.-manufactured durable goods saw a significant increase of 7.9% in April.

items.headline: Fed Faces Inflationary Pressure Amid Leadership Change
items.summary: Kevin Warsh was officially sworn in as the new Federal Reserve Chairman. Despite pressure for lower interest rates, high gas prices have pushed inflation to its highest level in almost three years, potentially making the Federal Reserve cautious about cutting rates. The probability of a Fed rate hike by December decreased slightly to 59% following softer PCE inflation data.

items.headline: Technology and Healthcare Lead, Energy and Industrials Lag
items.summary: On the previous trading day, healthcare, information technology, and consumer discretionary sectors recorded the biggest gains. Tech stocks are showing a strong comeback in May, with the State Street Tech Select Sector SPDR ETF (XLK) up 22.3% year-to-date. Conversely, energy and industrials stocks closed lower on Thursday. Healthcare, despite its significant economic contribution, is currently the most out-of-favor sector this year.

items.headline: U.S.-Iran Ceasefire Hopes Drive Oil Prices Lower
items.summary: Hopes for a potential U.S.-Iran ceasefire extension, aimed at reopening the Strait of Hormuz, have eased geopolitical fears and contributed to a decline in oil prices. Brent crude is on track for one of its biggest monthly drops, nearing a 17% fall since the start of May.

items.headline: AI Euphoria Continues, Raising Valuation Concerns
items.summary: AI infrastructure spending remains robust, with companies like Dell benefiting from increased demand for data centers. Private AI valuations are soaring, highlighted by Anthropic's valuation surging to $965 billion. However, the AI-fueled rally has pushed overall market valuations, such as the Shiller CAPE ratio, to levels last seen during the dot-com bubble, raising concerns among some analysts.
